data.title: The Nomination Committee for Humana AB's 2026 AGM has been constituted
data.description:
Humana AB

The members of the Nomination Committee for Humana AB's Annual General Meeting in 2026 have now been appointed.

According to the instructions adopted at the General Meeting Humana AB shall have a Nomination Committee comprising one representative for each of the four principal shareholders in terms of votes and the Chairman of the Board. In the event that a shareholder chooses to refrain representation in the Nomination Committee, if the Nomination Committee deems it appropriate, a representative of the next largest shareholder may be given the opportunity to join.

Based on the ownership structure as of August 31, 2025, the following individuals have been appointed to members of the Nomination Committee for the 2026 Annual General Meeting:

  • Fredrik Strömholm, representative from Impilo Care AB, Chairman of the Nomination Committee
  • Alexander Kopp, representative from Incentive AS
  • Anette Andersson, representative from SEB Funds AB
  • Richard Torgerson, representative from Nordea Funds
  • Anders Nyberg, Humana's Chairman of the Board

The Nomination Committee shall prepare proposals for the Annual General Meeting regarding the Chairman of the Annual General Meeting, number of Board members, fees to be paid to each of the Board members, election of Board members and Board Chairman, remuneration to the auditor and election of auditor and, if necessary, proposal for changes in the instruction to the Nomination Committee.

The Annual General Meeting of Humana AB will be held on Thursday, May 7, 2026.

Shareholders who wish to present proposals to the Nomination Committee for the 2026 Annual General Meeting can submit them through email to arsstamma@humana.se  or by post to Humana AB, AGM, Att: Ewelina Pettersson, Warfvinges väg 39, S-112 51 Stockholm. In order for the Nomination Committee to be able to consider submitted proposals in a constructive manner, these should be submitted by 8 January 2026, at the latest.

For more information, please contact:

Ewelina Pettersson, Head of Investor Relations

+46 73 074 79 12, ewelina.pettersson@humana.se

Humana is a leading Nordic care company providing services within individual and family care, personal assistance, elderly care and special service housing in accordance with LSS. Humana has 23,000 employees in Sweden, Norway, and Finland providing care for approximately 10,000 individuals and working to achieve the vision "Everyone is entitled to a good life". In 2024, Humana's net revenue was SEK 10,295m. Humana is listed on Nasdaq Stockholm and the company's headquarters are located in Stockholm. Read more about Humana on: https://www.humanagroup.com/

https://news.cision.com/humana-ab/r/the-nomination-committee-for-humana-ab-s-2026-agm-has-been-constituted%2Cc4239465

The Nomination Committee for Humana AB's 2026 AGM has been constituted

data.title: Morningstar advises selective approach on US utilities amid sector pullback
data.description:
** Morningstar says it thinks investors will have to be selective to find U.S. utilities that can produce consistent, long-term earnings growth that justifies their premium valuations

** Brokerage adds that utilities is the only sector down since July, despite a dovish interest rate turn

** It expects data center energy demand to triple in the next five years before energy efficiency and a shift to more inference, or predictive, models begin to slow data centers' energy use growth

** "We think mid-cap utilities in the Midwest have the best chance of realizing the earnings growth and return expectations that are priced into their stock" - Morningstar

** Brokerage adds that the market underappreciates the growth potential of top picks Alliant Energy and Duke Energy

data.title: Sugar Futures at Over 1-Week High
data.description:
Sugar futures hovered around 15.6 cents per pound, their highest since mid-September, mainly supported by rising prices of crude oil in international markets.

Higher crude prices boost ethanol demand, encouraging mills to shift cane from sugar to ethanol and curbing sugar output.

Still, sugar prices have trended lower over the past six months amid signs of abundant global supply.

StoneX released a revised projection on September 23, forecasting a global surplus of 2.8 million tons for the upcoming 2025/26 season, compared to a deficit of -4.7 MMT in the 2024/25 season.

data.title: Oracle dips after report co seeking to raise $15 billion from bond sale
data.description:
** Shares of Oracle fall 3.1% to $304.2, after Bloomberg News reports co is looking to raise $15 billion from corporate bond sale

** ORCL selling debt in as many as seven parts, including a rare 40-year bond, according a regulatory filing, though it did not disclose offering's size

** Oracle did not immediately respond to Reuters' request for comment

** 33 of 43 brokerages rate stock "buy" or higher, 10 "hold"; median PT is $347 - data compiled by LSEG

** Including session's move, ORCL up 82.8% YTD, while broader S&P 500 is up 13.3%

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0945 ET - The Czech National Bank left its key interest rate at 3.50% on Wednesday, as expected. Capital Economics expects the next move could be an increase. "Policymakers have continued to signal that conditions are not currently in place to loosen monetary conditions further," emerging Europe economist Nicholas Farr says in a note. CE expects unchanged rates through 2026, with a risk of a hike. Money markets, however, price a 42% chance of a rate reduction by December, LSEG data show. The Czech koruna is little moved after the decision, trading slightly weaker on the day at 24.280 a euro. It hit a 21-month high of 24.201 a euro Tuesday, LSEG data show. (jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)

0943 ET - Excluding the U.S. dollar, CAD remains the worst-performing major currency in 2025, and is likely to remain in a slump for the remainder of the year, says Karl Schamotta, chief market strategist at forex firm Corpay. Traders are souring on CAD because the Trump administration's tariff policy have exposed Canadian economy's structural weaknesses--like miserable productivity, high household debt and housing-market vulnerabilities. "We think this dynamic will remain in place through year end as growth remains moribund," Schamotta says, adding the Bank of Canada is likely to cut its policy rate at least one more time. Schamotta believes there might be room for recovery early next year, depending on new pro-growth policies from PM Mark Carney and possible trade resolution with Washington. (Paul.Vieira@wsj.com, @paulvieira)

0900 ET - Treasury yields go in different directions, as investors look more interested in shorter-term securities sensitive to Fed cuts while shunning longer-term risks. A sobering mood was set yesterday by Fed Chair Powell's remarks about policy risks. Futures are pricing another 25 basis point cut in October, while investors wait for inflation and labor data. Weekly jobless claims, due tomorrow, are expected to tick higher, in a WSJ survey. August annual PCE inflation, Friday, is forecast at 2.7%, up from 2.6%, with core steady at 2.9%. The 10-year yield adds a few basis point, to 4.129%, and the two-year slips slightly, at 3.578%. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0756 ET - Telecom Italia's latest bond issuance confirms the company is on a clear path toward financial improvement, Equita analyst Domenico Ghilotti writes in a note. The Italian telecommunications company issued a 500 million-euro bond at a 3.625% coupon, a move the analyst sees as further evidence of the company's strengthening finances. Additionally, the reduction in the average cost of debt is set to be a significant driver of enhanced free cash flow in the coming years, he says. Shares are flat at 0.43 euros. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

0742 ET - Business leaders in Canada are calling for artificial intelligence policy, regulation and incentives to be implemented, research by KPMG shows. A poll of 750 business leaders in late August and early September found that 92% want the federal government to set up a regulatory framework as soon as possible. And 89% reckon tax credits for small and medium-sized businesses to adopt AI would be a good start, while 91% say government must offer better incentives for workers to develop intellectual property and remain in the country. The poll also showed 88% believe Canada's new minister of AI will help Canada advance its position in AI and make the country more competitive, KPMG says. (robb.stewart@wsj.com)

0725 ET - Italy's new five-year benchmark government bond, or BTP, should offer a yield 4-5 basis points higher than the previous one, says Luca Cazzulani, head of strategy at the Investment Institute by UniCredit, in a note. The Italian treasury announced Tuesday the launch of a new 2.85% February 2031 BTP alongside the reopening of three other bonds at an auction Friday. The new 2031-dated BTP should be attractive relative to BTPs with maturities in 2029, as well as relative to April 2030- and August 2030-dated BTPs, Cazzulani says. Italy's 10-year BTP yield is up 0.7 basis points at 3.585%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0640 ET - U.S. Treasury yields fall in European midday trade. Speeches by Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell and Governor Michelle Bowman on Tuesday gave contrasting policy outlooks, says Brown Brothers Harriman's Elias Haddad in a note. Bowman suggested last week's rate cut would be the start of a series, while Powell was more cautious. "The risk is the Fed turns more dovish by the time of the December 9-10 FOMC meeting," Brown Brothers Harriman's senior markets strategist says. The two-year Treasury eases 0.7 basis point to 3.562%, the 10-year yield falls 1.4 basis points to 4.103%, while the 30-year yield is down 1.7 basis points at 4.719%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0638 ET - The Swedish krona looks set to strengthen by year-end as the Riksbank is likely finished cutting interest rates, ING analyst Francesco Pesole says in a note. The Riksbank unexpectedly cut rates by 25 basis points on Tuesday. However, the updated rate projections indicated the end of the cutting cycle. The euro could fall back below 11.00 by year-end, he says. "In the longer run, we agree with the Riksbank's assessment that the krona remains undervalued, and this rate cut does not materially change our downward-sloping forecast for [euro versus the krona] into mid-2026." The euro rises 0.1% to 11.0208 krona. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0637 ET - Malaysia's GDP is expected to grow 4.2% in 2025, with a strong 2H building on solid 1H momentum, RHB economists Barnabas Gan and Chin Yee Sian say. Growth will be supported by steady domestic demand, wage gains and continuing multiyear public and private projects. The economy is forecast to strengthen further in 2026, with 4.7% growth, underpinned by investment completion, higher capital deployment in strategic sectors and policies under the 13th Malaysia Plan, a roadmap for economic development. Private consumption is set to remain a growth anchor, buoyed by rising household incomes from civil service wage adjustments and a tighter labor market. While U.S. sectoral tariffs might weigh, the economists believe Malaysia's diversified services sector could cushion external shocks. (jason.chau@wsj.com)

0557 ET - Sterling looks vulnerable after Tuesday's U.K. purchasing managers' index survey missed expectations and pointed to a slowdown in economic activity, Monex Europe analysts say in a note. The downbeat survey "aligns with our view that the U.K. is on a faltering trajectory," they say. This could push the Bank of England towards cutting interest rates at a faster pace. The negative momentum from the PMI survey could persist and leave sterling at risk of falling, particularly if risk appetite sours, they say. Sterling falls 0.4% to $1.3474 against a stronger dollar. The euro falls 0.1% to 0.8729 pounds after reaching an almost two-month high of 0.8745 Tuesday, LSEG data show. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0541 ET - Singapore's headline inflation is expected to be subdued at 1.0% in 2025, in line with the Monetary Authority of Singapore's projections, say CGS International economists Mohamed Afham Zulghafir and Nazmi Idrus. After inflation moderated in August, CGS views the risks as balanced. While renewed shocks could drive up energy and shipping costs, softer global and domestic growth might keep core inflation low for longer. The economists add that the impact of inflationary pressures from global trade conflicts on Singapore should be cushioned by weaker external demand, which exerts a disinflationary effect. Domestically, government subsidies for essential services are also expected to help cap price increases in the services sector, they note. (jason.chau@wsj.com)

0513 ET - A weak economic growth outlook favors a slightly longer duration within bond portfolios, ODDO BHF's Laurent Denize says in a note. Bonds with longer duration are more sensitive to interest-rate changes. "Given the subdued economic growth in the U.S. and Europe, we believe a slightly longer duration makes sense," says Denize, who is co-CIO of ODDO BHF and CIO of ODDO BHF Asset Management. The asset manager has duration preference for U.S. Treasurys. The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield eases 0.8 basis point to 4.109%, while the 30-year Trasury yield is down 1.2 basis points at 4.725%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: NYAB signs agreements in Finland worth EUR 50-55 million
data.description:
NYAB AB

NYAB has signed two agreements with in the Energy market segment in Finland with a combined total value of approximately EUR 50–55 million. The contracts cover engineering, procurement, and construction services within NYAB’s Civil Engineering business segment. The projects will start immediately, with completion expected during 2027.

Revenues will be recognized progressively over the project period. The contracts follow standard market terms, including milestone-based payments, and are structured to support the Group’s cash flow profile.

Further details regarding the projects will be disclosed at a later stage, at the request of the customer.

For further information, please contact:

Erik Petersen, VP Corporate Affairs, Head of Communications and IR

NYAB AB

erik.petersen@nyabgroup.com

+46 (0) 70 880 42 23

About NYAB

NYAB enables the progress of society for future generations with extensive experience from complex and challenging projects. We provide engineering, construction, and maintenance services within infrastructure, industrial construction, and energy. Our main markets are Sweden, Finland, and Norway. We have approximately 1100 employees, and our stock is listed on Nasdaq First North Premier Growth Market Sweden. The group is headquartered in Luleå, Sweden.

data.title: U.S. Stocks Open Modestly Higher; Alibaba Stock Jumps — WSJ
data.description:
By Chelsey Dulaney

Artificial-intelligence optimism is boosting markets again-this time driven by China.

Chinese tech titan Alibaba upsized its pledge to spend on AI and released a new model, helping send its U.S.-listed shares higher. Other Chinese tech stocks that trade in New York also rose, including search-engine company Baidu and e-commerce giant PDD Holdings.

Alibaba's pledge to spend more than $53 billion on artificial intelligence and cloud infrastructure comes amid big investment announcements from U.S. chip maker Nvidia.

The spending boom has helped propel equities to record highs recently, offsetting concerns about job-market weakness, a potential government shutdown and questions over how quickly the Federal Reserve will cut interest rates.

Fed Chair Jerome Powell disappointed some investors Tuesday by steering clear of strong hints as to the bank's actions come October.

In recent trading:

Stocks opened modestly higher, led by the Dow Jones Industrial Average.

The dollar strengthened against the Japanese yen and the British pound in particular.

Oil futures extended gains after President Trump called on European allies on Tuesday to end energy purchases from Russia.

Write to Chelsey Dulaney at chelsey.dulaney@wsj.com

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Impala Nordic: Brain+ – First Ayla NHS pilot launch in UK primary care
data.description:
Impala Nordic comments the news that Brain+ A/S (“Brain+” or the “Company”) has signed an agreement to deploy Ayla in one of the UK’s largest primary care practices within the UK National Health Service (“NHS”). We view this agreement as a landmark step for Ayla’s UK adoption.

This will be the first deployment of Ayla, and the first use of Cognitive Stimulation Therapy (“CST”), in NHS primary care. The seven week pilot at a General Practitioner practice serving more than 28 000 patients is intended to demonstrate the benefit of introducing Ayla-delivered CST earlier in the pathway. A recent Post Market Clinical Follow-up Study (“PMCF”) pilot conducted by the Company in two UK care homes reported nearly 50 % improvement in cognitive outcomes. The agreement complements the primary near-term cash driver in private care homes, opening a long-term NHS growth channel, and it shows the Company’s ability to turn relationship-building into action.

Read our full comment here.

data.title: BEWI ASA: Cancellation of Subsequent Offering
data.description:
BEWI ASA

Reference is made to the stock exchange announcement made by BEWI ASA ("BEWI" or the "Company") on 20 August 2025 regarding the successful private placement raising gross proceeds of NOK 896 million (the "Private Placement"), and a potential subsequent offering directed towards eligible shareholders (as described therein) of up to 3,000,000 new shares in BEWI (the "Subsequent Offering"), at a subscription price of NOK 20 per share (the "Subscription Price"), equal to the price per share in the Private Placement.

Following the announcement of the completion of the Private Placement on 20 August 2025, more than 8.6 million shares (i.e. approximately 2.9 times the size of the Subsequent Offering) have been traded on Euronext Oslo Børs at or below the Subscription Price and with a volume-weighted average price (VWAP) per share of NOK 18.7. The latest closing price per share on Euronext Oslo Børs was NOK 17.6, i.e. well below the Subscription Price.  

Accordingly, any existing shareholder wishing to reduce the dilutive effect of the Private Placement has had

sufficient opportunity to purchase shares in the market at prices at or below the Subscription Price. On this background, the board of directors of BEWI has today resolved not to proceed with the Subsequent Offering.

For further information, please contact:

Charlotte Knudsen, Director of IR and Communications BEWI ASA, tel: +47 9756 1959

About BEWI ASA

BEWI is an international provider of packaging, components, and insulation solutions. The company's commitment to sustainability is integrated throughout the value chain, from production of raw materials and end goods, to recycling of used products. With a vision to protect people and goods for a better every day, BEWI is leading the change towards a circular economy.

BEWI ASA is listed at the Euronext Oslo Børs under ticker BEWI.

This information is subject to the disclosure requirements pursuant to section 5-12 of the Norwegian Securities Trading Act.

https://news.cision.com/bewi-asa/r/bewi-asa--cancellation-of-subsequent-offering%2Cc4239282
